commit.message:
feat(verification-gate): profile-aware evidence matching with canonical value normalization (#158)

- Export CANONICAL_REPORT_VALUES (frozen Set of pass/fail/n/a)
- Export normalizeReportValue() to canonicalize common synonyms
  (passing/passed/ok/yes/true → pass; failing/failed/no/false/error → fail;
   na/not-applicable/skip/skipped/exempt → n/a)
- Apply normalization in parseVerificationReport so values are canonical at
  parse time; truly unknown values pass through for gate-level detection
- Add non-canonical value gap for required fields: prevents false negatives
  where exotic strings (e.g. 'xyz') bypass the fail/n/a checks
- Track optional field failures in evidence.optionalFieldFailures[]
  (tracked for false-negative proxy calibration but never block the gate)
- Initialize optionalFieldFailures in base evidence object so early-return
  paths (missing report, skipped/partial status) always include the field
- Add optionalFieldFailures to VerificationEvidence type in worker_runner.ts
  and populate it from validationResult.evidence in the audit snapshot
- Add 30+ new tests covering normalizeReportValue, non-canonical gap,
  optional failure tracking, and integration paths through validateWorkerContract

commit.message:
feat(prometheus): reject high-risk low-confidence packets before normalization (#150)

Add checkHighRiskPacketConfidence() gate that rejects raw packets which are
explicitly riskLevel='high' but lack BOTH premortem AND acceptance_criteria.

The OUTPUT FORMAT prompt mandates premortem for high-risk plans. When both
confidence signals are absent the packet is an undocumented stub — dispatching
it is indistinguishable from random mutation on a critical path.

Gate runs in runPrometheusAnalysis() after the existing checkPacketCompleteness
gate, before normalizePrometheusParsedOutput(). Rejections are logged with
reason=high_risk_low_confidence and counted in _rejectedHighRiskLowConfidenceCount.

Only explicit riskLevel='high' triggers rejection; inferred risk is deferred
to normalization where full task text context is available.

Exports:
- checkHighRiskPacketConfidence(rawPlan) -> { requiresRejection, reason }
- HIGH_RISK_LOW_CONFIDENCE_REASON constant

16 new tests in prometheus_parse.test.ts covering positive, negative, and
boundary cases. Total test count: 3774 (was 3758), all passing.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/emmtrix/emx-onnx-cgen/sessions/90152413-23c3-4435-99da-fe53aaf74b57

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* lowering: fix LayerNormalization scale broadcast rank and RotaryEmbedding input order

LayerNormalization (_ensure_broadcastable):
- Relax scale/bias rank check: allow any rank <= input_rank, with broadcast
  compatibility checked right-aligned against the full input shape (not just
  the normalized sub-shape). This matches the ONNX spec which states scale/bias
  must be unidirectionally broadcastable to X.

LayerNormalizationOp.emit (nn.py):
- Compute scale_index_vars / bias_index_vars right-aligned with all loop vars
  (prefix + norm), so that scale tensors with leading batch/prefix dims work
  correctly in the generated C.
- Handle scalar scale/bias (rank 0) by returning ["0"] as the single index
  into the C array declared as [1].

RotaryEmbedding (rotary_embedding.py):
- Fix input order: the operator signature is input, [position_ids], cos_cache,
  sin_cache. The previous code assumed input, cos, sin, [position_ids].
- For 3D inputs without a num_heads attribute, infer num_heads from the
  cos/sin cache shape: head_size = rotary_dim = 2 * cos_shape[-1],
  num_heads = hidden / head_size.
- Allow position_ids of shape (1,) in addition to (batch, seq_len). Shape (1,)
  is a starting-position offset; the actual position for sequence index s is
  position_ids[0] + s.

rotary_embedding_op.c.j2:
- Emit pos = position_ids[0] + s for the broadcast (offset) case.

Update expected_error snapshots for 15 LayerNormalization and 12 RotaryEmbedding
test models whose error type changed from a lowering/codegen failure to a
numerical result (or pass).

commit.message:
fix: patch libtoolize to add missing LT_SYS_SYMBOL_USCORE macro

- Newer libtool versions removed the LT_SYS_SYMBOL_USCORE macro
- libffi's configure.ac still requires it
- Wrap libtoolize to inject the macro definition into m4/libtool.m4
- This allows autoreconf to find the required macro
